Intraday post (17/Feb)

Weekly post:

OpEx, VIXpery and PCE week | Weekly post (17-20/Feb)

Python quant course is already available on my Substack-site, by an ex-Wall Street quant.

Read it carefully.

For today, momentum is underpressure after overnight session. For ES, 6850/60 is the main pivotal area, a strong resistance, but for downside momentum 6835 must be taken out to be confirmed. Targets are 6790 and 6750/60For any upside move to be confirmed, 6905 must be taken out, with target of 6920/30.

For today, momentum is underpressure after overnight session. For ES, 6850/60 is the main pivotal area, a strong resistance, but for downside momentum 6835 must be taken out to be confirmed. Targets are 6790 and 6750/60For any upside move to be confirmed, 6905 must be taken out, with target of 6920/30.

Let’s look at the daily positioning…

INTRADAY POST Coding today’s positioning:

The speed profile is net negative, and it is more about defending the 6805.78 line. Below, it is more like flat. Upside vvol is sold.

Centroid is at 6775.06Today, this is pretty much a reversion level. A very strong support.

Downside pivot is at 6757.09target is 6715.04The target is a rejection level, while the pivot is a local magnet/stabilizator.

Upside pivot is at 6822.26target is 6886.79The pivot is a high probability rejection-line. While the target could be a stabilizator, if spot reaches it, but very low probability. When rejection happens, 6840 can be a local magnet if 6830/25 holds, but low probability.

The majority of the bet is about a hovering around 6805.78, between the centroid and the upside pivot. (I would add an upside margin of error of 6845/50). Watch futures price actions for confirmation.
